expectations.text: Phase 1 asks user to explain Bethe-Bloch Formula in their own words
expectations.evidence: Output: 'Explain [[Bethe-Bloch Formula]] in your own words. Don't look at the note — just tell me what you know.'

expectations.text: Phase 1 feedback identifies key omissions (beta-gamma dependence, MIP, corrections)
expectations.evidence: Identifies: incomplete beta-gamma vs speed, missing charge z dependence, vague MIP description, missing dE/dx notation, missing density effect and shell corrections, missing TPC application

expectations.text: Phase 1 generates exactly 3 follow-up probing questions
expectations.evidence: Three follow-up questions: (1) why beta-gamma not KE, (2) why different species separate, (3) density effect correction

expectations.text: Phase 2 generates 5 adversarial questions with correct type distribution (2 trick, 2 cross-concept, 1 exam-style)
expectations.evidence: Q1 Trick (momentum misconception), Q2 Trick (same charge different mass), Q3 Cross-concept (truncated mean + ionization), Q4 Cross-concept (ToF + dE/dx PID), Q5 Exam-style (derive functional dependence)

expectations.text: Cross-concept questions reference actual wikilinks from the Bethe-Bloch Formula note
expectations.evidence: Q3 references [[Time Projection Chamber]] and [[Ionizing Radiation]], Q4 references [[Time-of-Flight]] and [[Particle Identification]] — all wikilinks present in the note
English Note section after Phase 1 feedback: 'Your response is grammatically correct. One small improvement...'

summary: All 6 assertions pass. Full mode followed all 5 phases correctly with appropriate question types and wikilink-based cross-concept synthesis.
